_note: Floor values to prevent display regression. update_tokscale.py ratchets these up whenever SSR returns higher values, and applies them as a minimum when SSR returns lower (proportionally scaling per-(client,model) breakdown).
_recovery_methodology: Multi-event compensation. (a) 2026-04-21..2026-05-06: server data eroded by naive `tokscale submit` calls on rotated local logs. Estimated loss $3,029 / 3.22B tok / 28,323 msgs absorbed into floor on 2026-05-12. (b) 2026-05-14 10:29 KST: a one-time `submit -c claude` (with TOKSCALE_FORCE_LOSS=1) that was expected to leave the synthetic `reconstructed-claude-history` bucket intact in fact destroyed ~94% of it ($12,455 -> $790, ~$11,665 erased) — the CLI's submit semantics turned out to be CLIENT-level REPLACE, not (client, model)-level. Floor was ratcheted to (pre-submit total $49,986 + Claude growth $7,721) = $57,707 to absorb the loss visually. safe_submit.py now BLOCKS any client whose server bucket holds a synthetic/recovery model unless TOKSCALE_FORCE_SYNTHETIC_DELETE=1 is explicitly set.
_initial_source: Recovered baseline 2026-05-12 via reverse-calc from .tokscale-update.log; updated 2026-05-14 after reconstructed-claude-history erasure event.
